channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Iowa gives final approval to a bill removing gender identity protections despite massive protests
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Iowa lawmakers became the first in the nation to approve legislation removing gender identity protections from the state’s civil rights code Thursday, despite massive protests by opponents who say it could expose transgender people to discrimination in numerous areas of life.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oscar-winner Gene Hackman, wife Betsy Arakawa and their dog were dead for some time, warrant shows
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — Oscar-winner Gene Hackman, his wife and one of their dogs were apparently dead for some time before a maintenance worker discovered their bodies at the couple's Santa Fe home, investigators said.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN and BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Students escape school bus fire in Ohio
item.description: An emergency broke out Thursday morning on a school bus carrying children in Cleveland Heights, Ohio. An unexpected blaze — requiring a quick evacuation.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Refugee aid groups say Trump administration is trying to circumvent court order
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Refugee aid groups said in a federal court filing Thursday that President Donald Trump’s administration appears to be trying to circumvent a ruling this week that blocked his efforts to suspend the nation’s refugee admissions program. U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Woman suspected in Colorado Tesla dealership vandalism charged in federal court
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Federal prosecutors have charged a woman in a string of vandalism against a Colorado Tesla dealership, including throwing Molotov cocktails at vehicles and spray painting “Nazi cars” on the building along with a message that appeared directed at company co-founder Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: College students create dating app with safety as top priority
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) -- As if college students didn’t already have enough on their plates with classes and studying, a group of students at Ohio State came together to create a new app called DuoDate.
item.dc:creator: Gracie Collins, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mexico sends drug lord Caro Quintero and 28 others to the US as officials meet with Trump team
item.description: Mexico has sent infamous drug lord Rafael Caro Quintero to the United States along with 28 other prisoners requested by the U.S. government.
item.dc:creator: Fabiola Sánchez And Joshua Goodman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Texas says this doctor illegally treated trans youth. He says he followed the law
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (AP) — On the Texas border, Dr. Hector Granados treats children with diabetes at his El Paso clinics and makes hospital rounds under the shadow of accusations that have thrown his career into jeopardy: providing care to transgender youth. In what's believed to be a U.S.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: No big new revelations in Justice Department’s release of Jeffrey Epstein files
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi has released government documents related to wealthy financier Jeffrey Epstein, but the first wave of files posted on the Justice Department website have largely been circulating in the public domain for years and didn’t include any new bombshells about the sex trafficking
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Gene Hackman, wife Betsy Arakawa were dead for some time, found in different rooms, investigators say
item.description: A spokesperson for the state’s medical examiner declined to comment on whether any conclusions had been reached on the cause.
item.dc:creator: Susan Montoya Bryan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FDA cancels meeting to choose flu vaccine strain
item.description: The cancellation of the FDA meeting, alongside a similar postponement last week of a meeting of Centers for Disease Control and Prevention vaccine advisors, is adding to the growing concern among scientists and public health advocates that Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: Gayle King, Katy Perry, Lauren Sánchez to board 'historic' Blue Origin space flight
item.description: "CBS Mornings" co-anchor Gayle King, singer Katy Perry and Lauren Sánchez will be among an all-woman crew blasting off on Jeff Bezos's Blue Origin as part of what's being called an "historic" space flight.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KRON4

item.title: MAP: Listeria strain in deadly outbreak found in these states as death toll climbs
item.description: A listeria outbreak tied to supplement shakes is now linked to at least 12 deaths and 38 cases across 21 states, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WGN 9

item.title: Oscar-winner Gene Hackman, his wife and their dog found dead in their New Mexico home
item.description: The gruff-but-beloved Hackman was among the finest actors of his generation, appearing as both villains, heroes and antiheroes in dozens of dramas, comedies and action films from the 1960s until his retirement in the early 2000s.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Republican lawmakers look for ways to weaken state judges
item.description: TOPEKA, Kansas (AP) — Measures in several state legislatures this year have called for new approaches to weaken the power of judges. One would abandon a centuries-old precedent that courts can decide whether laws are constitutional. Another would change how judges are selected.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Utah is one step away from becoming 1st state to ban fluoride in water
item.description: The Utah governor's signature is all that stands between a state ban on water fluoridation after House Bill 81 cleared its final hurdle in the legislature on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Drachman, WGN 9

item.title: New details released in Lake Oswego shooting that damaged SAIF CEO's home
item.description: Authorities are seeking information about a suspect believed to have fired multiple rounds into the home of the CEO of an Oregon workers' compensation insurance organization, causing damage to the front door and minor damage to the home.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Plante, NewsNation

item.title: Six-time deported gang member wanted for murder busted in Texas illegal immigration sting
item.description: An reputed MS-13 gang member in the U.S. illegally, who had been deported six times, was arrested in Texas Monday after allegedly murdering a Houston man.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bell tolls as New York marks the anniversary of the 1993 World Trade Center bombing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City on Wednesday marked the anniversary of the 1993 bombing at the old World Trade Center, when Islamic extremists first attempted to bring down the twin towers with 1,200 pounds of explosives in a parked rental van.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MINCHILLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Super Bowl sports reporter found dead in hotel had sedative in his system: authorities
item.description: The woman accused of taking the reporter's phone and bank cards has previously been accused of drugging men to steal from them, according to investigators.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Hamilton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mystery illnesses claim over 50 lives in Congo: What we know
item.description: Unidentified illnesses in northwestern Congo have killed more than 50 people over the past five weeks, nearly half of them within hours after they felt sick.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Kentucky woman accused of marrying her biological father
item.description: A paternity test claimed the probability that Kimberly Tempel and Bradly McCollom were father and daughter were greater than 99.99%
item.dc:creator: Zach McKnight, NewsNation

item.title: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy expected to sign minerals deal Friday: Trump
item.description: Details are vague but what is known is that the United States is requesting revenue from Ukraine's rare minerals.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court hears arguments on straight woman's 'reverse discrimination' case
item.description: Marlean Ames filed suit against a state agency claiming she was not given a promotion because she is heterosexual.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hackers slam Michigan tribe for not negotiating after ransomware attack
item.description: A group called RansomHub has claimed responsibility for the cyberattack and submitted a letter to provide its “side of the events.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, NewsNation

item.title: Judge weighs continued freedom for Adnan Syed in 'Serial' murder case
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — More than a decade after Adnan Syed’s murder case attracted massive interest through the hit podcast “Serial,” a Baltimore judge is considering a request to reduce his sentence to time served, which would allow him to remain free indefinitely even though his conviction still stands.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Democratic governors pitch state jobs to federal workers cut by Trump
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — Some of America's governors — mostly Democrats — have a message for the wave of fired federal workers: We want you.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Christianity in US stops decline after years of losses: Survey
item.description: Although down substantially from 2007, 44 percent of Americans said they pray at least once a day, a figure that has remained consistent in the last four years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to rule for Ohio woman claiming job bias because she's straight
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court seemed likely Wednesday to side with an Ohio woman who claims she suffered sex discrimination from her employer because she is straight.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Michelle Trachtenberg, 'Gossip Girl' and 'Buffy' actress, dead at 39
item.description: Actress Michelle Trachtenberg has died, police sources confirmed to multiple news outlets on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Woman accused of marrying her biological father
item.description: A Tell City woman is facing charges in Vanderburgh County for allegedly marrying her biological father.
item.dc:creator: Zach McKnight, WFLA, NBC

item.title: American Airlines flight discontinues landing to avoid departing plane in Virginia
item.description: The maneuver occurred less than two hours before another plane attempting to land at Chicago’s Midway Airport was forced to climb back into the sky to avoid another aircraft crossing the runway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Child becomes first measles death reported in Texas outbreak
item.description: An unvaccinated school-aged child who was hospitalized with measles has passed away, marking the first death in the outbreak, according to Lubbock and state health officials.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Rooney, KRON4

item.title: Trump pitches 'gold card' as replacement for EB5 investor visa
item.description: The Trump administration will be revamping the U.S. investor visa, increasing the funds required to secure the status while dubbing the new option a "gold card.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: Diana Taurasi of the Phoenix Mercury retires after 20 WNBA seasons, 3 titles and 6 Olympic golds
item.description: Diana Taurasi is retiring after 20 seasons, ending one of the greatest careers in women's basketball history.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MARSHALL and DOUG FEINBERG AP Basketball Writers, WFLA, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Officers cut open man's 'narco wig' at Colombian airport to reveal cocaine
item.description: He may have been using his head, but a 40-year-old passenger wasn't able to get past Colombian airport security with a cocaine-stuffed "narco wig," police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court orders new trial for Oklahoma death row inmate
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday sided with an Oklahoma death row inmate who claimed alongside the state itself that his trial was unfair, ordering a new trial and staving off his execution.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5
